name: Sanada Yukimura
description: Sanada Yukimura, born Sanada Nobushige, was a renowned samurai warrior of the Sengoku period, celebrated as one of the greatest generals in Japanese history. He was a member of the Sanada clan, a powerful family in Shinano Province, and distinguished himself through his exceptional military prowess and unwavering loyalty. Yukimura is most famous for his defense of Osaka Castle during the Siege of Osaka in 1614-1615, where he led a small force against the much larger army of Tokugawa Ieyasu. His innovative tactics and courageous leadership inflicted heavy casualties on the Tokugawa forces, earning him the respect of his enemies and solidifying his legendary status. He is often referred to as 'The Crimson Demon of War' or 'A Hero Who May Appear Once in a Hundred Years,' owing to his fierce determination and strategic brilliance. Although ultimately defeated and killed in battle, Yukimura's legacy as a symbol of bravery, military genius, and unwavering loyalty to his lord continues to inspire generations of Japanese people. He embodies the spirit of the samurai code and is a prominent figure in Japanese folklore and popular culture, appearing in numerous books, plays, and video games. Yukimura's story is a testament to the power of courage and skill in the face of overwhelming odds, making him a timeless hero of Japan.
subtitle: The Crimson Demon of War
achievements: Inflicting heavy losses on Tokugawa Ieyasu
Becoming a symbol of samurai loyalty

keyMyths.title: Defense of Osaka Castle
keyMyths.description: Sanada Yukimura's most famous feat was his defense of Osaka Castle against Tokugawa Ieyasu. He constructed a formidable fortress known as the Sanada-maru and repelled numerous attacks from the Tokugawa army.
keyMyths.source: Various historical accounts and fictionalized narratives

keyMyths.title: Crimson Demon of War
keyMyths.description: Yukimura earned the nickname 'Crimson Demon of War' due to his fierce fighting style and the red armor he often wore. This symbolized his courage and willingness to fight to the death for his cause.
keyMyths.source: Edo period folklore and historical records

keyMyths.title: The Last Stand
keyMyths.description: Despite being outnumbered, Yukimura fought valiantly until the very end during the Summer Campaign of Osaka. His death on the battlefield cemented his status as a heroic figure in Japanese history.
keyMyths.source: Historical accounts of the Siege of Osaka

sources.author: A.L. Sadler
sources.work: The Maker of Modern Japan: The Life of Tokugawa Ieyasu
sources.description: A biography of Tokugawa Ieyasu, which includes information about the Siege of Osaka and Sanada Yukimura.
sources.text: A biography of Tokugawa Ieyasu, which includes information about the Siege of Osaka and Sanada Yukimura.

sources.author: Stephen Turnbull
sources.work: Osaka 1615: The Last Battle of the Samurai
sources.description: A detailed account of the Siege of Osaka, focusing on the military strategies and key figures involved, including Sanada Yukimura.
